parameters.content:
## README
- Owner: Richard Berenczei / richard.berenczei@pwc.com
- Status: **Draft** — not yet activated for production use
- Dependencies: IMAP credential, BIG - Expose Processing Core sub-workflow
- Logic Summary: Poll the configured inbox for new emails, extract all PDF attachments (one item per PDF), normalize each into the shared intake contract, and dispatch to the extraction core.

## Input Contract
- **Trigger:** IMAP email poll with `downloadAttachments: true`
- No programmatic input — watches a mailbox automatically

## Output Contract
Returns one Processing Core result per PDF attachment found in the email.

## Normalized Handoff to Core
`{ source_filename, ingestion_channel: "email", received_date, duplicate_mode: "overwrite", persist_result: true, persistence_mode: "postgres", email_subject, email_from }` + binary key `pdf`

## Error Behavior
- Silently skips non-PDF attachments (no error, just filtered out)
- If an email has zero PDF attachments, no items are emitted
- Downstream errors are handled by the Processing Core and the global Error Handler (PIlbEFexoMxalpwB)

## Known Limitations
- `duplicate_mode` is hardcoded to `overwrite` — no per-email override
- Emails with multiple PDFs produce independent pipeline runs with no shared context

## Trigger / Input
Blue section: poll the configured inbox and download message attachments for intake automation.

## Success Path
Green section: filter to PDFs, emit one item per attachment, and dispatch the normalized payload to the core workflow.
